title: APIv2 (python) tests: fix flake
body:
Python tests were flaking because they behave differently
when $DEBUG is set. It looks like something in CI sets
that envariable.

Solution: do not use $DEBUG as a debug trigger, use a
properly-named custom variable that is unlikely to be
set accidentally.

Also: get rid of AssertTrue(), which gives no visibility
into what happened. Write in proper form that can emit
useful diagnostics on failure.

Fixes: #10948

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

/hold

Looks like my fix worked. I need to resubmit this with the `.cirrus.yml` changes removed (those were to avoid running lots of jobs). I will wait until #10947 merges, rebase, and remove the `Skip()`.

@edsantiago disabling the debug seems to be good solution, but maybe better would be to demux stdout/stder:
```diff
diff --git a/test/python/docker/compat/test_containers.py b/test/python/docker/compat/test_containers.py
index 38ac5b59f..7c5a52454 100644
--- a/test/python/docker/compat/test_containers.py
+++ b/test/python/docker/compat/test_containers.py
@@ -226,10 +226,12 @@ class TestContainers(unittest.TestCase):
 
             buff.seek(0)
             ctr.put_archive("/tmp/", buff)
-            ret, out = ctr.exec_run(["stat", "-c", "%u:%g", "/tmp/a.txt"])
+            out: bytes
+            err: bytes
+            ret, (out, err) = ctr.exec_run(["stat", "-c", "%u:%g", "/tmp/a.txt"], demux=True)
 
             self.assertEqual(ret, 0)
-            self.assertTrue(out.startswith(b'1042:1043'), "assert correct uid/gid")
+            self.assertEqual(out, b"1042:1043\n", "assert copied file has correct uid/gid")
 
             ret, out = ctr.exec_run(["cat", "/tmp/a.txt"])
             self.assertEqual(ret, 0)
```
@matejvasek that's much more pythony than I could've ever done; I will include it in my next push. Thank you!
I included it in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/10949 but maybe I'll close the PR.

And, on second thought, I'm not going to do that. I'm really neurotic when I test: I want to do exact matches, not "close enough". There is no circumstance in which we should accept anything other than `1042:1043`. There is no circumstance in which we should accept anything on stderr. Anything on stderr should trigger a test failure.
